data.title: Shift4 Payments announces Isaacman’s resignation as CEO
data.description:
Shift4 Payments’ (NYSE:FOUR) Jared Isaacman has resigned from his role as the Chief Executive Officer, the payments processor said on Wednesday.

The company named Taylor Lauber as Isaacman’s successor and principal executive officer, effective June 5, 2025. Isaacman has been appointed as the Executive Chairman of the company.

Lauber’s appointment is part of the company’s previously announced succession plan. He has also been appointed to serve as a Class I member of the board, with a term to expire at the 2027 annual meeting of stockholders.

Earlier in the week, it was said that Isaacman is expected to continue as Shift4 Payments' CEO after President Donald Trump withdrew his nomination of the executive as the NASA administrator over the weekend. Last year, Trump nominated Isaacman, who's also an astronaut, to lead the NASA.

The company’s shares fell marginally in extended trading on Wednesday.

data.title: D-BOX Technologies appoints Naveen Prasad as interim CEO
data.description:
• D-BOX Technologies (TSX:DBO:CA) announced on Wednesday a mutual agreement with Sébastien Mailhot, its chief executive officer (CEO), under which Mailhot will be stepping down, and Naveen Prasad will assume the role of interim CEO, effective as of the close of business on June 10, 2025.
  • In addition, D-BOX announced the appointment of Lori Vaudry Tersigni as an independent director.
  • The corporation further announced that it expects to release its financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year ended March 31, 2025, on June 10, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dynavax responds to proxy advisor calling for board change
data.description:
Dynavax (NASDAQ:DVAX) said that a recommendation from proxy advisory firm Glass Lewis against two of its director nominees is wrong.

"Significant Board composition and strategy changes have already been made, and the directors needed to continue successfully executing our superior strategic plan that is delivering significant long-term stockholder value are already on the Board," the company said in a news release.

"Indeed, Glass Lewis acknowledges that Dynavax's 2019 pivot has generated significant stockholder returns, the Company's share repurchase program differentiates Dynavax amongst our peers, and our Board has been meaningfully refreshed with high-quality and sector-specific expertise."

Deep Track Capital, which owns nearly 15% of the outstanding shares, has been highly critical of Dynavax's stock performances and has called for board change. It has recommended its own slate of four nominees.

"After evaluating the arguments presented by each side in this proxy contest, we believe that Deep Track has highlighted valid and critical questions regarding Dynavax’s strategic direction, capital allocation, and board responsiveness," Glass Lewis wrote, per a news release from Deep Track. 

"In light of our findings, we believe that shareholders should support the change of two incumbent board seats given the Company's prolonged capital conservatism and the board’s limited responsiveness to shareholder feedback," the firm added. 

In May, competing proxy advisor Institutional Shareholder Services took a different perspective and recommended that shareholders vote for all four of the company's director nominees.

Dynavax's annual shareholder meeting is June 11.

data.title: RadNet completes deal with See-Mode
data.description:
• RadNet (NASDAQ:RDNT) said on Wednesday that it completed the acquisition of See-Mode Technologies, an AI innovator for ultrasound imaging. 
  • RadNet's DeepHealth population health solutions will  incorporate See-Mode's commercially available AI-powered ultrasound detection, characterization, and reporting solutions for the thyroid and breast.

data.title: Gold jumps on sluggish U.S. economic data, adding to safe-haven demand
data.description:
Gold futures gained Wednesday, helped by weaker than expected U.S. economic data and a softer dollar, on top of the tariff uncertainty and geopolitical tensions that have been driving safe-haven demand for the metal.

The Institute for Supply Management said its non-manufacturing purchasing managers index dropped to 49.9 in May, contracting for the first time in a year, while ADP data showed U.S. private employers added the fewest workers in more than two years.

The safe-haven aspect of gold has been further enhanced by the weakening of the U.S. dollar, and the U.S. dollar index fell 0.5% on Wednesday, making gold cheaper for buyers holding other currencies.

President Trump signed the executive order raising tariffs on steel and aluminum to 50% from 25%, and he posted that his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping was tough and "extremely hard to make a deal with," just days after accusing China of violating an agreement to roll back tariffs, adding to pessimism that any potential U.S.-China trade compromise is on the horizon.

Central banks also continue to add to their reserves, albeit at a slower pace in April, and analysts at ING said central banks likely will continue adding to their holdings in response to the uncertain economic environment and efforts to diversify away from the U.S. dollar.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery ended +0.7% to $3,373.50/oz, its second gain in the past three sessions, and front-month June Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.1% to $34.519/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Newmark facilitates $210 million sale of office tower in Los Angeles
data.description:
• Newmark Group (NASDAQ:NMRK) announced on Wednesday the company has arranged the sale of 601 South Figueroa Street, a 1,041,315-square-foot high-rise office tower in Downtown Los Angeles.
  • The asset sold for $210 million, equating to a price of approximately $202 per square foot, said the company. 
  • At the time of sale, the building was 72% leased to tenants from diverse industries, with many representing strong national and regional credit profiles. Professional services firm PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) serves as the anchor tenant.
  • NMRK -1.68% after hours to $11.07.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Circle raises $1.1B in upsized IPO, prices shares above marketed range - report
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (CRCL) and some of its backers pulled in $1.1B in an upsized initial public offering as the stablecoin issuer priced its shares above the marketed range, according to a media report published on Wednesday.

In the offering, the company and some of its selling shareholders, including co-founder and CEO Jeremy Allaire, sold 34M shares for $31 each, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

An earlier U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing showed the offering was marketed at $27-$28 a share after being boosted once before amid strong demand.

That would give Circle a market value of $6.9B and a fully diluted valuation of ~$8.1B, the article said, based on the outstanding shares disclosed in the filing.

data.title: Corus Entertainment appoints John Gossling as CEO
data.description:
• Corus Entertainment (TSX:CJR.B:CA) announced on Wednesday changes to its management leadership team, including the appointment of John Gossling as chief executive officer (CEO). 
  • Formerly co-chief executive officer and chief financial officer, Gossling first joined Corus in 2016.
  • "In light of the evolving industry landscape as well as the actions taken to date to stabilize the capital and debt structure of the company, the Board has decided to transition back to a single CEO structure," said Mark Hollinger, Lead Independent Director and Co-Chair of the Human Resources and Governance Committee. 
  • With these changes, Troy Reeb has decided to pursue opportunities outside the company and has stepped down as Co-CEO, said the company. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Travelers files automatic mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) filed automatic mixed securities shelf.
  • Filing

data.title: Starbucks appoints Mike Grams as COO
data.description:
Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) appointed North America chief coffeehouse officer Mike Grams to the position of chief operating officer, said CEO Brian Niccol on Wednesday.

In addition to leading the company’s North American coffeehouses, Grams, 55, will now oversee global coffeehouse development and global supply chain, Starbucks said. Grams joined Starbucks in February 2025, after nearly thirty years at Taco Bell, where he held various positions, including President and COO.

“We’re bringing the Global Coffee and Sustainability team and the Global Brand team together. Coffee is at the heart of Starbucks, and this change will further cement coffee at the core of our menu, product innovation, and marketing,” Niccol said, adding that Michelle Burns, executive vice president of global coffee and sustainability, will report to Tressie Lieberman, the company’s chief brand officer.  

Additionally, Deb Hall Lefevre, executive vice president, chief technology officer, will now report to CFO Cathy Smith, while chief legal officer Brad Lerman will leave the company.

“We’re on the right path, but we need to move faster. We’re making additional organizational changes to help bring teams closer together, strengthen leadership accountability, and allow teams to move with even greater clarity and urgency,” Niccol added.

Niccol, who took the realm last year, said he wants Starbucks stores to be a welcoming coffeehouse where people gather and where the company serves the finest coffee handcrafted by skilled baristas, leading to a number of changes in policies.

The Seattle-based coffee chain operator’s shares were little changed in extended trading on Wednesday. Overall, they fell over 5% so far this year, compared to the 1.5% rise in the broader S&P500 Index.
